ecosystem_summary.last_notable_event: Unit 42 (Palo Alto Networks) published analysis confirming persistent malicious skills on ClawHub post-VirusTotal integration (Feb-May 2026)

newly_noted_skills_or_sources.name: Skill Vetter (clawhub)
newly_noted_skills_or_sources.note: Ranked #1 overall — scans skills for red flags. We already do this manually with our local skill-vetter skill.

newly_noted_skills_or_sources.name: Ontology Memory
newly_noted_skills_or_sources.note: Persistent long-term memory across sessions. Interesting but we already use file-based MEMORY.md approach.

newly_noted_skills_or_sources.name: Felo Search
newly_noted_skills_or_sources.note: AI-optimized web search with citations — we already have Tavily for this.

newly_noted_skills_or_sources.name: Veryfi (OCR)
newly_noted_skills_or_sources.note: OCR/document processing skill — could be useful for receipt/invoice processing if needed.

newly_noted_skills_or_sources.name: mcporter
newly_noted_skills_or_sources.note: Teaches agents to interact with MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers — bridge skill.
newly_noted_skills_or_sources.action: potentially useful if we adopt MCP tools, monitor

newly_noted_skills_or_sources.name: ClawScan + NVIDIA analysis
newly_noted_skills_or_sources.note: ClawHub now runs NVIDIA analysis tool on all skills and has ClawScan code analysis. Not a user-installable skill.

security_alerts.Unit_42_update: Palo Alto Networks found 5 unblocked malicious skills post-VirusTotal integration (Feb-May 2026) — 3 threat categories leveraging AI supply chain
security_alerts.ongoing_risk: Despite VirusTotal + ClawScan, malicious skills still slip through. Always vet before install.

installation_recommendations.reason: Document/OCR processing — install if need arises for receipt/invoice/PDF data extraction
MCP protocol bridge — emerging trend, worth watching but not urgent

verdict: Ecosystem at 13,700+ skills, growing fast. No new must-install skills surfaced today. Latest security finding from Unit 42 confirms persistent supply chain risks despite platform countermeasures. Current skill set remains solid for our needs. Recommend checking back weekly.
